models.label: Qwen3.6 35B A3B (24GB Q4_K_M, think off)
models.note: PRIMARY for single-shot workloads (briefing, narrative, chat, tool-call). 17-18/18 on judgment set, 4/4 PASS on real cds-engine prompts. Think mode is ON by default for this model and adds ~80s of hidden chain-of-thought latency; MUST keep think:false in production.
caveats: Without think:false, briefing latency is ~108s vs ~15s.

AGENT CAVEAT: with permissive prompts it skips tool calls and fabricates answers (2/6 PASS on agent tasks). Use strict 'MUST call tools before answering' system prompts for agent use (then 3/3 PASS). For multi-turn tool loops prefer gemma4:26b unless prompts are defensively written.
One-shot iv-crush run produced a 'safely outside short strikes' hallucination; did NOT reproduce across 3-run verification.

models.label: Gemma 4 26B A4B (17GB MoE, 3.8B active)
models.note: PRIMARY for multi-turn agent loops (6/6 PASS on agent scenarios with permissive prompts, no hallucination on data-absent cases). Also 6/6 on short synthetic judgment tasks with best-calibrated confidence.
caveats: Timed out (>180s) on real cds-engine one-shot prompts with uncapped output - NOT suitable for the briefing/narrative path. Use Qwen3.6 for those.
num_predict: 1024 produced empty output (thinking-channel issue); avoid num_predict caps.
Verbose at default sampling (~3000+ tokens on one-shot prompts vs Qwen3.6 ~500).
Safe for: multi-turn agent loops (short individual turns), short synthetic reasoning. Not safe for: one-shot long production prompts.

models.label: Qwen3 8B dense (5.2GB, fast fallback)
models.note: Fast fallback for RAM-constrained or latency-critical paths. 4.5/6 on judgment set; competitive with the 35B MoEs on short workloads.
caveats: Less disciplined than the 35B MoEs on ambiguous judgment; fell for 'iron condor on conflicting signals' trap.
Prefer only when qwen3.6:35b latency or RAM is prohibitive.
